completion_promises.criterion_text: tests/integration/test_mode_b_c_crash_recovery.py covers Group D Scenario Outlines verbatim — Mode B: crash during feature-spec, feature-plan, or autobuild → restart → build re-enters preparing state → prior in-flight stage is reattempted from the start (no partial-progress replay)
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeBRetryFromScratchAcrossNonTerminalStages parametrises over (feature-spec, feature-plan, autobuild). Each row seeds prereq stage_log rows up to but not including the in-flight stage on a real SqliteLifecyclePersistence-backed tmp_path database, drives the build through QUEUED → PREPARING → RUNNING, closes the writer connection (crash), opens a fresh connection, runs reconcile_on_boot, asserts BuildState.INTERRUPTED, drives INTERRUPTED → QUEUED → PREPARING, and runs ModeBChainPlanner against the real durable history to assert plan.next_stage equals the in-flight stage. A separate assertion confirms no duplicate ‘approved’ stage_log row was written for the re-attempted stage.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Mode C: crash during task-review or task-work → restart → build re-enters preparing state → prior in-flight stage is reattempted from the start
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeCRetryFromScratchAcrossNonTerminalStages defines two tests: test_crash_mid_task_review_replays_initial_review (no review row pre-crash → planner returns next_stage=TASK_REVIEW with rationale ‘initial’) and test_crash_mid_task_work_replays_same_fix_task (approved review with one fix task ‘FIX-X1’ pre-crash → planner returns TASK_WORK with FixTaskRef.fix_task_id == 'FIX-X1'). Both use real SqliteLifecyclePersistence and exercise reconcile_on_boot.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Durable-history authority (ASSUM-009): asynchronous Mode B autobuild in flight → runtime crashes → on restart authoritative status read from durable history; live async_tasks state channel data is treated as advisory; the autobuild is re-dispatched as a fresh asynchronous task with a new task identifier; the previous task identifier is recorded as abandoned in stage history
completion_promises.evidence: TestDurableHistoryAuthorityModeBAutobuild stages a Mode B build in RUNNING with FEATURE_SPEC + FEATURE_PLAN approved on real SQLite, simulates a crash + reconcile_on_boot, records the original autobuild task_id ‘autobuild-task-1001’ as abandoned via _record_abandoned_task (uses sentinel stage_label ‘crash-recovery-abandoned-task’ so planner projection skips it), drives PREPARING, asserts ModeBChainPlanner returns AUTOBUILD as next_stage, simulates a fresh dispatch with new task_id ‘autobuild-task-2042’, and asserts (a) abandoned task list equals ['autobuild-task-1001'] and (b) the only AUTOBUILD stage_log row's task_id equals the new value.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Same durable-history-authority assertion holds for an in-flight Mode C task-work
completion_promises.evidence: TestDurableHistoryAuthorityModeCTaskWork mirrors AC-003 for Mode C: writes a TASK_REVIEW PASSED row with fix_tasks=('FIX-MC-1',), simulates the crash, records original_task_id='taskwork-task-700' as abandoned, runs ModeCCyclePlanner against real durable history, and asserts plan.next_stage is TASK_WORK with FixTaskRef.fix_task_id == 'FIX-MC-1'. Abandoned task ID is verified in stage history via _read_abandoned_task_ids.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Cycle-state preservation in Mode C: a crash during the third of five fix tasks reattempts the third fix task from the start (not the first); the prior two completed /task-work entries remain in stage history with their approved status preserved
completion_promises.evidence: TestModeCCycleStatePreservationAcrossCrash seeds a Mode C build with TASK_REVIEW emitting fix_tasks=('FIX-1','FIX-2','FIX-3','FIX-4','FIX-5'), writes PASSED TASK_WORK rows for FIX-1 and FIX-2, leaves FIX-3 mid-flight (no row), simulates crash + recovery on real SQLite, then asserts (a) sorted approved fix_task_ids equals ['FIX-1', 'FIX-2'] (prior approvals intact) and (b) ModeCCyclePlanner.plan_next_stage returns TASK_WORK with FixTaskRef.fix_task_id == 'FIX-3' (third reattempted, not first).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Approval-channel isolation across crash: if Build A is paused at a flagged-for-review checkpoint when the runtime crashes, the resume after restart still routes the approval response to Build A only (build-identifier routing is durable, not in-process state)
completion_promises.evidence: TestApprovalChannelIsolationAcrossCrash creates two Mode B builds A and B in real SQLite, drives A to PAUSED with persistence.mark_paused(build_a, 'approval-req-A-DURABLE'), drives B to RUNNING, simulates crash + reconcile_on_boot with a RecordingApprovalPublisher, then asserts: (a) A remains PAUSED while B becomes INTERRUPTED, (b) report.paused_reissued_count == 1 and report.interrupted_count == 1, (c) exactly one approval envelope was published, (d) the envelope's payload.request_id equals the verbatim 'approval-req-A-DURABLE', and (e) the envelope's details.build_id equals build_a (not build_b) — proving build-identifier routing is durable.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Cancel during async crash recovery: a forge cancel issued against a build whose autobuild was interrupted by a crash resolves the build to cancelled terminal without re-dispatching the autobuild
completion_promises.evidence: TestCancelDuringAsyncCrashRecovery stages a Mode B build with a pre-crash AUTOBUILD GATED row carrying task_id='autobuild-task-9000', simulates the crash, runs reconcile_on_boot (build → INTERRUPTED), records the abandoned task_id, then drives the cancel path INTERRUPTED → QUEUED → CANCELLED via apply_transition. Asserts (a) final BuildState.CANCELLED, (b) exactly one GATED AUTOBUILD row exists (no fresh re-dispatch), (c) that row's task_id equals the original 'autobuild-task-9000', and (d) the abandonment audit row records the original task_id.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Tests use the actual SQLite persistence layer (no in-memory shortcut) so the crash-recovery contract from FEAT-FORGE-001 is exercised end-to-end
completion_promises.evidence: Every test in the suite opens a real on-disk SQLite database via tmp_path / 'forge.db', runs forge.lifecycle.migrations.apply_at_boot to apply schema.sql + schema_v2.sql, instantiates SqliteLifecyclePersistence, and routes every state transition through forge.lifecycle.state_machine.transition + persistence.apply_transition. Crash simulation closes the writer connection and reopens a fresh one against the same on-disk file. TestActualSqlitePersistenceLayerExercised adds two canary checks: (a) test_sqlite_persistence_facade_is_the_persisted_writer asserts the file exists and schema_version is at v2, (b) test_no_in_memory_stage_log_double_in_use asserts no DurableStageLog/AdvisoryAsyncTaskChannel/FakeStageLog classes are defined in the test module (the in-memory shortcut the AC forbids).

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: The project's pyproject.toml does not configure ruff, black, or any other lint/format tool ([tool.ruff] / [tool.black] sections are absent; the venv ships pytest only). The new file compiles cleanly via py_compile and follows the existing codebase conventions (PEP 8, type hints, docstrings, max line length ~88, snake_case helpers, PascalCase classes). No project-configured lint check exists for this run to violate.
